scenario: Helix Logistics runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS and processes 12000 shipments per day. An on-call operator opens Operate, clicks a deployed process name from the Process Instances by Name panel, and needs to cancel one specific stuck running instance directly from that process view without leaving the page.
question: Which action on the Processes tab cancels that single running process instance for the operator?

options.text: Clicking a bulk cancel button at the top of the Processes tab cancels every running instance of the selected deployed process in one Operate action
Clicking the version selector on the Processes tab cancels every running instance bound to the older versions of that deployed process model
Clicking the cancel icon under Operations next to the instance row on the Processes tab cancels that single running process instance
Clicking the deployed process name in the Process Instances by Name panel a second time cancels the most recently started running instance

a.text: Incorrect. The source describes cancelling a single running process instance from the Processes tab by clicking a per-row cancel icon under Operations, not by using a bulk cancel control that acts on every running instance of the deployed process.

b.text: Incorrect. The version selector filters the Processes view by process definition version; it does not cancel any running instance of older versions of the deployed process model.

c.text: Correct. The source states that from the Processes tab an operator can cancel a single running process instance by clicking the cancel icon under Operations next to the process instance row.

d.text: Incorrect. Clicking the deployed process name in the Process Instances by Name panel navigates to the process view that lists running instances; it does not cancel any running process instance.

explanation: On the Processes tab opened from the Process Instances by Name panel, each running process instance row exposes an Operations column with a cancel icon. Clicking that icon cancels that one running instance for the operator without affecting other running instances of the same deployed process definition.
